# Cost Forecast — 2026-06-27

**Subscription:** Azure subscription 1 (`ae5e40cd-96ef-48d8-950a-2e22cf8f991a`)
**Currency:** GBP
**Baseline window:** 2026-06-20 → 2026-06-26 (7 full post-Go-migration days; survivor run-rate)
**Generated by:** /cost-forecast

> **Caveat — read first.** The Cosmos → Postgres cutover completed **today (2026-06-27)**: Cosmos billed £0 for the first time, and the new Postgres server has **zero observed cost** (provisioned 2026-06-26; Cost Management data lags 24–48h). So the single largest line in this forecast — Postgres — is **rate-card-derived, not measured**. Treat the next-30/quarter numbers as a well-grounded estimate to be reconciled against actuals on the next run (~48h), when the first Postgres billing line lands.

## Headline

| Horizon                    | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta    | Confidence |
|----------------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|---------:|------------|
| Rest of month (27–30 Jun)  | £4.06              | £0.35          | +£3.71   | Medium     |
| Next 30 days               | £30.42             | £69.73         | −£39.31  | Medium     |
| Next quarter (90 days)     | £91.26             | £130.09        | −£38.83  | Low        |

**TL;DR:** Spend is trending **sharply down**. The Cosmos → Postgres cutover replaces a **~£32/mo-and-rising** Cosmos line with a **~£13.50/mo fixed** Burstable Postgres server — a **~58% cut to the database bill** and the single biggest cost event in the project's history. New steady-state is **~£1.01/day ≈ £30.9/mo**. Azure's own forecast is wrong in *both* directions: it **understates** the next few days (anchored on the artificially-low partial actuals of 26–27 Jun) and **overstates** the next 30–90 days by ~£39 (still extrapolating dead Cosmos history, plus a spurious £7.43/day spike across 12–21 Jul). The migration *was* the cost optimisation; the footprint is now near its practical floor.

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

- **Cosmos (`cosmos-town-crier-shared`) — destroyed today, still in Azure's projection.** Cosmos was the dominant cost: **£56.41 over the 60-day window**, £32.15 in the last 30 days alone (up from £24.25 the prior 30 — it was *growing*). Daily actuals: ~£1.5/day on 24–25 Jun → £0.61 on 26 Jun (cutover) → **£0.00 on 27 Jun**. The destroy is confirmed: no `Microsoft.DocumentDB` resource remains in the subscription. Azure's linear model still carries ~£1/day of Cosmos forward — that is the bulk of its £39 over-statement on the 30/90-day horizons. Commit `26b1604a` (2026-06-27, "destroy shared Cosmos account").
- **Postgres (`psql-town-crier-shared`) — new, invisible to Azure.** Provisioned 2026-06-26 (`9e4e159c`), cut over 2026-06-27 (`9bb09651`). Zero cost in MTD because (a) it is two days old and (b) cost data lags. Azure's forecast inherits 25 days of Postgres zeros and projects ~£0/day for it. Bottom-up correctly prices it from the rate card at **£0.445/day**. This is why Azure *understates* the near term — but it is swamped on the longer horizons by the Cosmos drag and the mid-July artifact.
- **The £7.43/day spike, 12–21 Jul (Azure forecast only).** Azure's model invents a 10-day plateau at £7.43/day — a seasonality/confidence-band artifact extrapolating June's Cosmos growth ramp and dual-run churn. It has no basis in any current resource. Strip it and Azure's curve roughly agrees with bottom-up. This single artifact accounts for essentially all of the 30-day over-statement.
- **Old .NET apps already gone.** `ca-town-crier-api-prod` (£1.35/60d) and `ca-town-crier-api-dev` (£0.68/60d) both billed **£0 for the last 7 days** — decommissioned mid-June when Go took over. Correctly excluded from forward run-rate.

The Container Apps line is inflated by the June dual-run (.NET + Go both live, plus migration churn). Its forward run-rate is the *Go-only* survivor figure below (~£13/mo across api + jobs), not the £8.45 last-30 number.

## Bottom-up breakdown by resource

Survivor run-rates are the mean of 7 full days (20–26 Jun), excluding Cosmos and the dead .NET apps. Postgres is rate-card (B1ms £0.0142/hr × 730 + 32 GB × £0.0991/mo). 27 Jun is excluded everywhere as a partial day (~25% booked).

| Resource | Service | Daily run-rate | Rest-of-month (4d) | Notes |
|----------|---------|---------------:|-------------------:|-------|
| `psql-town-crier-shared` | PostgreSQL | £0.445 | £1.78 | **NEW / LOW CONFIDENCE** — rate-card, 0 days actuals. B1ms Burstable, 32 GB, no HA, no geo-backup. Verify on next run. |
| `ca-town-crier-api-go-prod` | Container Apps | £0.245 | £0.98 | Stable; minReplicas=1 (warm prod, deliberate). ~£7.5/mo. |
| `acrtowncriershared` | Container Registry | £0.124 | £0.50 | Basic, fixed daily charge. 87 MB / 10 GB used (0.8%) — no overage. |
| `job-tc-poll-prod` | Container App Job | £0.092 | £0.37 | PlanIt polling worker. Essential. ~£2.8/mo. |
| `ca-town-crier-api-go-dev` | Container Apps | £0.079 | £0.32 | minReplicas=0 (scales to zero). Cost is request-driven activation + deploy churn. ~£2.4/mo. |
| `job-tc-poll-bootstrap-prod` | Container App Job | £0.011 | £0.04 | Seed/bootstrap poll. |
| `job-tc-digest-hourly-prod` | Container App Job | £0.009 | £0.04 | Hourly digest. |
| `job-tc-digest-hourly-dev` | Container App Job | £0.005 | £0.02 | Dev hourly digest. |
| `log-town-crier-shared` | Log Analytics | £0.004 | £0.02 | At 30-day min retention; near-zero ingest. |
| `appi-town-crier-shared` | App Insights | ~£0.004 | £0.02 | Folded into Azure Monitor line. |
| `acs-town-crier-uk` | Email (ACS) | ~£0.001 | £0.003 | Transactional email. |
| Other jobs / SB / Storage / SWA | — | ~£0.003 | £0.01 | dormant-cleanup, subscription-sweep, daily digest, Service Bus, blobs, static web apps — all ~£0. |
| **Total** | | **£1.014** | **£4.06** | Survivors £0.569/day + Postgres £0.445/day. |

## Recommendations

The honest headline: **there are no material new cost-reduction opportunities.** The migration already executed the big one (−£18.5/mo on the database line), and the surviving footprint is already at its sensible floor:

- Postgres is the **cheapest Burstable tier** (B1ms), 32 GB, no HA, no geo-redundant backup. (Note: Azure **does not offer Reserved Capacity for the Burstable tier** — only General Purpose / Memory Optimized — so there is no reservation discount to capture here.)
- Dev and prod **share one** Postgres server, one ACR, one managed environment — already consolidated.
- ACR is Basic at 0.8% of the 10 GB quota; Service Bus is Basic; Log Analytics / App Insights at the 30-day minimum. Nothing to trim.

The only conscious cost lever left untouched is **`ca-town-crier-api-go-prod` minReplicas=1** (warm prod, ~£5–6/mo of the £7.5/mo). This was a *deliberate* decision (killed cold-start ahead of go-live; superseded earlier min=0 attempts). **Not recommended for reversal** — it is a UX choice the owner has already made, flagged here only for completeness.

Actions taken instead of new beads (dedup / hygiene):

- **Updated `tc-0qnj`** (P4, "Postgres/RG cost budget to replace removed Cosmos-spend alert") with the new baseline. A subscription-level **£50/mo budget already exists** (`budget-subscription-monthly`); steady-state run-rate ~£30.9/mo sits at ~62% of it — healthy headroom. A per-RG/Postgres budget remains a nice-to-have, not urgent.
- **Closed three Cosmos cost beads** made obsolete by the destroy: `tc-tm5o` (evaluate Cosmos throughput tuning), `tc-cdp3` (set Cosmos backup to Local), `tc-8gi5` (profile Cosmos RU). The resource no longer exists.
- **`tc-fxau`** already owns the dev revision sprawl (47 active revisions today). Correctly assessed as **£0 direct cost** (dev scales to zero) with a weekly Monday cleanup; left as-is.
- **Orphan `id-town-crier-cosmos-data` managed identity** — leftover from the Cosmos decommission. **£0 cost** (managed identities are free), so no cost bead; flagged here as drift, belongs with the existing Cosmos-teardown fast-follow (`tc-hpd2.15`).

## Methodology footnote

- **Data window:** 60-day actuals (2026-04-28 → 2026-06-27), daily, grouped by ResourceId and by ServiceName (Cost Management query API). Azure forecast: 90-day horizon (forecast API). Postgres rates: Azure Retail Prices API (GBP, uksouth).
- **Survivor baseline:** mean of 20–26 Jun (7 full days), excluding Cosmos and the two dead .NET Container Apps. 27 Jun excluded as a partial day (Cost Management lag; ~25% booked).
- **Key exclusions (one-offs / dead):** all Cosmos spend (destroyed); old .NET apps (already £0); migration backfills (completed, per epic #645 / memo 0010 — no forward one-offs expected).
- **Known gaps:** (1) **Postgres has zero observed cost** — the £0.445/day figure is rate-card, not measured; first billing line expected 2026-06-28/29, reconcile then. (2) Pulumi `stack export` not run this pass; the live `az resource list` (35 resources) was used as ground truth, cross-checked against the cutover commits — no untracked/drift resources of material cost found. (3) Quarter horizon assumes flat traffic; real user growth or PlanIt ingestion changes will move it.
